description: This figure presents the relationship between the logarithm of the equilibrium constant (logK), the logarithm of the actual concentration product (logQ), and the solid fraction (zs) for AlN inclusions in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels with varying aluminum contents (x = 1, 3, 5 wt%, labeled as 1#, 2#, and 3# respectively). The solid curves represent logK values while the dashed curves represent logQ values, with black, blue, and red colors denoting samples 1#, 2#, and 3# respectively. The intersection points of the logK and logQ curves indicate the solidification fractions at which AlN inclusions begin to precipitate: 0.46 for sample 1#, 0.22 for sample 2#, and 0.51 for sample 3#. The graph demonstrates that increasing Al content from 1% to 3% advances the precipitation of AlN from a solid fraction of 0.46 to 0.22, whereas further increasing Al to 5% delays the precipitation to 0.51. This non-monotonic trend reflects the complex influence of Al content on the solidification path, liquidus/solidus temperatures, and microsegregation behavior of Al and N elements during solidification, as calculated using thermodynamic models (FactSage 8.2) and the Clyne-Kurz microsegregation model.
